definition: Superior ROIC does not protect concentrated market leaders against discount rate shocks. Nifty Fifty companies had ROE >20% and genuine advantages but fell 50-70% from pure multiple compression when the inflation regime shifted. Fundamental quality ensured recovery but not drawdown prevention.
evidence: Nifty Fifty stocks fell 50-70% from multiple compression despite strong fundamentals
Companies survived and thrived but drawdowns were severe
Pair_0 debate: both generalists converge that Mag7 ROIC addresses earnings pathway but not discount rate pathway
Conditional: if AI productivity is real, 1995-2000 regime holds and precedent doesn't activate
ROIC differential (>25% today vs 20% in 1972) is meaningful
open_questions: Does the current ROIC differential (>25% vs 20%) materially change drawdown magnitude?
What inflation regime shift would be the catalyst?
Is AI productivity sufficient to sustain the 1995-2000 regime?
